name: SaaS Landing One-Pager
description: A refined SaaS or B2B one-page marketing page focused on value, proof, features, and conversion.
usage: Use for product-led marketing pages, waitlists, B2B feature narratives, and conversion-focused one-pagers.
best_for: Product-led marketing pages, waitlists, B2B feature narratives, and conversion-focused one-pagers.
Briefs that need hero, value proposition, feature proof, and CTA sections.
avoid_for: Social long images where all content must fit a mobile screenshot flow.
Pure data reports that need chart-first analysis.
layout_rules: Use a direct first viewport with product name, value proposition, proof point, and primary CTA.
Follow with 3-5 focused sections: problem, workflow, feature proof, use cases, and closing CTA.
Use restrained product panels or diagrams only when they clarify the offer.
Keep navigation minimal or omit it for single-file artifact delivery unless requested.
style_rules: Use a quiet professional palette with one brand primary, cool or warm neutrals, and one accent.
Use crisp typography, subtle section bands, 1 px borders, and low-contrast shadows.
Avoid oversized decorative hero copy, stock-like illustrations, and dense nested cards.
Make buttons and links accessible with visible focus states.
content_rules: Turn vague benefits into concrete outcomes without inventing numbers.
Keep CTA labels specific when supported by the brief.
If proof is missing, use source-grounded mechanisms rather than fake testimonials.
quality_checks: The hero should explain what the product does within a few seconds.
Repeated sections should not become identical feature cards.
The page should feel like a finished marketing artifact.
